title: nephrodium rufum
intro: Nephrodium rufum, also known as the Rusty Oak Fern, is a species of fern native to North America, characterized by its rust-colored fronds and rhizomes. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Nephrodium rufum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Nephrodium rufum are typically made by drying and powdering the fronds, which can be used in teas, tinctures, or applied topically as a poultice.
